reviews.review_description: It a cash grab they are bots the 5 starts don’t get scammed and the tutorial is impossible the fans didn’t work giving it 1 but I can’t whit 0 just the worst experience they not then roon it

Okay, so I got a code for Umbrella Up VR, and honestly… I was not expecting to have this much fun. The premise is simple but clever: you climb and swing using an umbrella. Sounds weird? Yeah, it kind of is—but in the best way.
The gameplay is quirky and honestly a bit chaotic at first. Learning how to maneuver with the umbrella takes a few tries, but once you get it, it feels super satisfying. I found myself laughing a few times just from the ridiculousness of some of the climbs.
Visually, the game is bright, colorful, and fun. The environments feel imaginative, and even though it’s not hyper-realistic, it has a charm that works perfectly for this type of game.
Controls can be a little tricky if you’re new to VR, but the challenge adds to the fun rather than being frustrating.
Overall? If you like VR games that are a little silly, a little challenging, and a lot of fun, this is worth checking out

developer_response.body: Thanks for your comment and love !

reviews.review_description:
Refund crowbar climbers and buy this instead! 

This game has so much more to do and it even has a couple gamemodes so you dont get bored!
And if thats not enough, it gets good updates!




ps: But there is room for improvement like easier movement and stuff like that.

Movement sucks, you roll uncontrollably, no option’s menu, annoying music that you cant turn off, annoying pop up tutorials, umbrellas dont do what you want, they often glitchand either get stuck or phase through things, over all do no buy ts, worst 15 cents of my life

developer_response.body: Sorry for the inconvenience! We’ve fixed the issues u mentioned: movement is more controlled, u can turn off the annoying music, pop-up tutorials are improved, and umbrella glitches are gone. Thx for the feedback, hope u give the game another go!

reviews.review_description: The climbing mechanic is great, sometimes you get stuck but that’s part of the charm. Also love the many different minigames spread out all over the levels. Can’t wait to see the multiplayer mode added as that would open up the game to a lot of shenanigans

Umbrella Up takes the familiar climbing genre and gives it a refreshing twist: you climb using umbrellas in each hand, which you can rotate to walk or open to catch wind lifts. It’s clever, fun, and surprisingly strategic. The precision of the climbing mechanics is a real highlight — responsive and satisfying, which makes every grip and reach feel earned.

The atmosphere is engaging with dynamic music, and there's plenty of vertical terrain to explore. Multiplayer is on the horizon, and it’ll be a fantastic addition for friendly challenges. This one’s a fresh breeze in the climbing genre — lots of reasons to keep reaching higher.

Honestly such a good game! I had a lot of fun playing it and the controls are quite original. However, some things like the movement and the lack of a difficulty curve are definitely flawed. As long as these things are improved however, this is a 5 star game!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the awesome feedback! 🙏 We’re so glad you enjoyed the game! We’ve already been working on improving the movement and difficulty, so stay tuned for some updates. With your input, we’re aiming for that 5-star experience! 🌟 Appreciate your support！

reviews.review_description: Climbing with an umbrella is something I definitely never thought of. The experience in this game is fantastic!

I got a chance to play "Umbrella Up" & fell in love with the game; the mechanics are quite smooth, the stage is colorful and always changing with new areas to explore that expands the world entirely, the music is nice, and the sense of achievement when you reach the destination. 

GAMEPLAY! 
The mechanics are smooth compared to other climbing sims. The umbrellas are an unusual tool but make so much sense as you use the umbrella in wind currents to reach different areas or heights then using the hook to climb bars give the umbrellas a nice multi-function! I didn't have any problems with the umbrellas crossing or getting stuck on one another, the transition between bar to bar, zip line, or wind currents are fluid. 

MAP! 
The map is very expansive with multiple areas to explore and interact with such as a jump pad 50 feet in the air! a slide, along with a pool area where you can interact with multiple items. The stages are colorful, there are characters in the game that'll follow you with their eyes which is thought was silly in a good way! To climb through the map top reach the top of a statue on a hill was a thrill as you had to be cautious not to fall down, then when you reach the top, you'll see the world evolve and unravel a new adventure to set upon. 

MUSIC! 
For a solo adventure game music is important! Nonetheless the music in the game is pretty nice, and relaxing, wasn't annoying at all, I hope for more music such as LoFi music in future updates. 

CONTENT TO COME! 
I am aware there is a multiplayer mode in the works and honestly cannot wait to see this update come to life as I'll love to race against others in the world! 

I cannot wait to see the future of Umbrella Up! 
Keep up the good work guys!

reviews.review_title: Best climbing game ive played in a while
reviews.review_description: Very good game, love how they fixed the movement from being flimsy and the player models are AMAZING

reviews.review_title: More Fun Than I Was Expecting
reviews.review_description:
I was told this was like crowbar climber which didn't leave a good impression on me as that game was too challenging to be fun for me, so I went in not expecting much and to my surprise I had a lot of fun!

 It is immediately more gentle and seemingly better optimized, plus, you have umbrellas that you can use to fall slower or even fly in some parts, the variation makes it less prone to get boring and speaking of that, I only went straight to the top which rewards you with extra stuff that appears in the end, but there are so many other parts I didn't even explore so there is a lot here for people who like a challenge or just mess around. I was also told there is multiplayer coming?! That will make it 100 times more fun.

reviews.review_title: This game has a lot of potential!!
reviews.review_description: Umbrella Up VR is a charming and creative flying game that surprised me in the best way. The umbrella mechanics are fun and intuitive, offering both casual fun and skill-based challenges. I love the colorful low-poly environments, though there’s room to improve the visual details. Overall, it’s a relaxing yet engaging experience that keeps me coming back to improve my flying. Well done, Monstarai! Would love to see more detailed environments in future updates :)

The level designs are peak and the game runs greatly, I love how the game works it’s content and mechanics and I just get excited when I remember that there is more content coming, my only complaint is the movement with the barrel under you, the climbing is smooth and all but when you traverse without climbing it’s kind of weird so if you guys could fix that it would be great!

If you enjoy climbing games, you’ll love the unique challenge of using two umbrellas to get higher and higher.
It’s fun, weird, and super satisfying when you finally make progress.

It’s still in early access, but the developers are clearly listening to the community — can’t wait to see how the game grows!

A great looking 'climbing' game. This time your hands are replaced with umbrellas. These function both for locomotion (which needs a bit of work) and climbing. The physics of the umbrellas is very good if a bit annoying when they get tangled. Mixed in with the climbing are some areas to investigate and play around in. I did find the accuracy of the movement annoying as I fell off the statue a lot. And I'm not sure why I needed to collect the stars anyway?

I expected this game to be a quirky and cartoony climbing simulator with some silly umbrella mechanics, but what I didn't expect was a full on vertical sandbox with multiple paths and all kinds of bonus activities and fun to explore areas that make you feel like you are a kid in a very large playground.

While admittedly I found some of the activities to have not been fully fleshed out, and they didn't all feel as great as I know they could potentially be, this is an early access game from a small dev team and it was just much larger in scope than I expected it to be, so it all feels like a bonus. 

The actual climbing mechanics and obstacles are fun, and offer just the right amount of challenge and frustration. Sometimes you will need to quickly hook onto something or fall to your demise, and that is how it ought to be. The climbing is challenging, unlike the climbing in most other climbing games, you need to be ready to react fast after sliding down a rail or flying to another path with your umbrella.

I like what this game is serving, and it exceeded my expectations, at least in the early access stage.

This game is really fun and quite challenging sometimes, but i love the ways that i have to control my character in different parts of the game. Definetly worth checking out if you’re into parkour vr games.

I Have a small Youtube Channel (Crash Course VR Games) and got chance to try this game out in early access.  Umbrella Up is Creative, Entertaining and at times frustrating but in the best of ways.  I found myself having a blast with the Unique concept of climbing with the Umbrellas, Using them to fly up with wind drafts and catching myself (sometimes not) and continuing the journey.  I have issues with Heights and some VR games can be hard on me at times, this games heights didn't upset me, but the long falls got me lol, but it didn't stop me from continuing.  I enjoyed the colorful aesthetics and peaceful little tune playing.  I'm looking forward to the multiplayer (I want to play this with my son) and see how multiple players can interact and change up the formula a bit.  There are mini games scattered throughout the level and is a nice break from the rest of the main concepts of Umbrella Up.   The movement (walking) is a bit different and takes a moment to get used to,but is also part of the charm. 
If you enjoy climbing games then this should definitely be part of your catalogue of games, and if you're looking for something different and a bit of a challenge (but very doable), then give it a shot.

reviews.review_title: Honest review FUN BUT NOT FOR ME
reviews.review_description:
Very cool idea and with updates im sure i could up my 3 stars to 5 stars. imagine frosty the snowman as an avatar with two umbrellas and your goal is to make it to the top like getting over it.
The mechanics are creative, you flip the umbrellas to climb with the hooks or flip again to open the umbrellas to catch updraft like in legend of zelda BOTW. Its cool its fun but watch my video to see why i give it 3 stars LOL

Only Up… But With Umbrellas in VR ☂️ (Gone Wrong)

reviews.review_title: Umbrella Up is an absolute blast!
reviews.review_description:
Umbrella Up is an absolute blast!

This game hooked me from the first launch. The style is super fresh and vibrant, with a playful twist that feels right at home in VR. It’s easy to pick up but stays fun and challenging the more you play. The controls are smooth and intuitive — no frustration, just straight-up fun.

I love how the game balances skill and quick reactions with a chill vibe that keeps you coming back for “one more run.” You can tell the devs put a ton of care into getting the feel just right. Whether you’re a VR veteran or new to the headset, Umbrella Up is a must-play — fun, polished, and full of personality. Highly recommend grabbing it!

I love the art in this game. It's a very adventurous creation with lots of fun surprises. Once you get the controls/movements down, you're good. The first time I did a super jump and then opened my umbrella to glide, I was like ok, "this is cool". I'm excited for future additions and updates. 

Great job devs, 👏 it's a winner!!!

Not only is the climbing fun, but the umbrella mechanic is very creative! I love the realistic-style backdrop, and there are plenty of inventive climbing sections. I hope Quest Achievements are added, maybe even just one for reaching the top! Great work!

The music that plays just give you that feeling to not give up and to keep going up! The maps and details just bring the mood! I love how some parts there are short cuts and ways to maneuver around stages. This game is a great start and cool game from “getting over it”. The water park is especially my favorite! I love that everywhere i look there’s always something interesting or a new shortcut. But when i made it to the end it was worth the view! Overall this game is an 10/10!

I LOVE this game, so much fun, fantastic and entertaining there’s always lots of challenges to do, it is tricky but worth it, keep going!!! You`re a snowman (of course) hanging out over Rio de Janeiro, grab your umbrella and head UP!!!! If you`ve not seen them then check out my videos of this game on YouTube!

https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLVgIqDTu766jaz4evnDrPPzYqvsf_7-Fw

Miri ❤️

this game is the best one recommendation is a menu to change what button to open the umbrella because some people have stick drift that is really bad and even if they put a dead zone, the dead zone gets restarted automatically but it’s still calibrated fire game tho

reviews.review_title: A Great Game, just needs more variety
reviews.review_description:
I had a pretty good time with Umbrella Up! The gameplay is simple but addictive—you control an umbrella through levels, solving puzzles and avoiding obstacles. The controls are easy to pick up, and the visuals are nice and clean, giving off a fun, light vibe. It’s one of those games that’s just chill to play, you know?

The difficulty is on point. It starts easy and gradually gets tougher, but never to the point where it feels impossible.

That said, after a while, things do start feeling a little repetitive. The mechanics don’t really change much, so the levels begin to feel similar. It’s still fun for a bit, but I found myself wanting something new to mix it up. I also ran into a few minor glitches here and there, but nothing that totally messed up the game.

Overall, Umbrella Up is a solid, fun game for short sessions. It’s not groundbreaking, but if you’re into puzzles or just need something casual to kill time, it’s definitely worth checking out.

reviews.review_title: Fun VR Gem That Floats Above the Rest
reviews.review_description:
Equal parts relaxing, funny, and surprisingly challenging, Umbrella Up has been a fun surprise so far. It takes the familiar locomotion and climbing mechanics and puts its own twist on it (along with floating mechanics) and turns it into a game that is part platforming, part puzzle-solving and part exploration.

There’s something satisfying about catching the gust of wind just right and avoiding obstacles in vr.

The game gradually introduces new mechanics that keep things interesting also. Check it out

The only things that you need for this game, are 2 umbrellas. Use them to climb and glide, or use them to fly higher with help of the wind. 

Collect stars on your way to the top.

 Watch out that you don’t fall, or you have to start all over again… 

The graphics are great. Just try it. You gonna like it. (Hope you are not afraid of heights)

At the start it’s hard but once u learn the mechanics of teh game and find the fun it it its amazing truly ashtonishing I recommend 100% it cures my border and its really fun to have a joke in but beware if u drop form w great hewight u will get really mad haha
Its just getting used to it but other than that its sick i love jumping as high as i can. Pls make respawn time quicker 🙏🙏
Crowbar climber but cheaper and with more stuff! I enjoyed playing and also thanks to the devs for giving me a game key

I would change a lot but quite good for a early access. I still think you NEED to get this game because of multiple things. IT IS A W ONLY UP WITH A LIV CAM. Listen the price is WORTH. (I would change the movement when not climbing.)

Dom from thevrsector here,  this is a very fun addictive game ,  could do with a few tweaks here and there which the developers will be making over time.  If you like climbing games amongst other things ,  this game is for you .  https://youtu.be/Isjx3_gzNm4?feature=shared
It has flaws. The movement is still not perfect but they are aware. I feel like this game has potential to be good. It is in early access but still has notable qualities.
A very unfortunately shorter ‘Getting over it’ style experience. Nice bit of physics and many alternative routes. The only down side is the length and the lack of ragebaiting in multiplayer sessions.

I love the physics in this game it's amazing and I love how  u climb with the umbrella i honestly wish they made a couple settings to turn down music and to turn off spawns so it can feel more harder with no spawns

This game offers a solid experience overall. The graphics are clean and well-designed, with enough detail to keep things visually interesting without being overwhelming. Controls are responsive and easy to learn, making gameplay smooth even for beginners.

Umbrella Up on Quest 3 feels a bit rough around the edges, but there's clear potential here. With only two devs behind it, the ambitious design is genuinely impressive-just expect it to take time to fully bloom.  With time, it's going to steal hearts 💕

I love the game. Very fun. The way you can glide using your umbrellas is very challenging and enjoyable. It’s has many levels and they’re all very unique. Can’t wait for multiplayer and skins to be added!

It’s a good game but the waking mechanic is a little difficult to use but the climbing part is immaculate it over all a fun game just gotta fix the walking other than that it’s a five star game

I’ve shared this game with several friends and family, and everyone enjoys it. We all have fun climbing the obstacles and the experience of virtual heights is thrilling. I’m looking forward to a multiplayer addition.
Bright, colorful and with good physics, I'm waiting for multiplayer mode as soon as possible
This game is golden. I like the design And it’s in Brazil. That makes you feel like you’re climbing real things￼
I love how this game has so many creative mechanics that adds that touch of uniqueness you need, and I think that it’s really amazing of how they thought of this.

As soon as I played it, I was goated, it is honestly the best game I’ve ever played! This is worth the price! There is clear instructions so u know what you are doing and make sure to go play the game right now⭐️

After the simulation game of climbing using a big hammer, then a crowbar, another climbing game that uses an umbrella as a tool, this is quite creative.
